food_subgroup: Carbonated soft drinks and flavoured sodas

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai iced green soda. FOODON_03301710 (carbonated beverage) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000A (Soft drinks) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Thai-style green soda.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific flavoured carbonated beverage product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one glass of 300 ml, equivalent to 300 g, as typically served in Thai restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, fruit-flavoured (generic); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Item 14148 and analogous Thai-style sweetened soda estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Thai Iced Green Soda is a sweetened, fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink coloured green, typically made with carbonated water, sugar syrup, and flavouring. No exact HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for this specific product. Nutrient values are estimated using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks (approximately 39–42 kcal per 100 g, ~10 g sugar per 100 g, negligible protein and fat), consistent with typical Thai-style sweetened sodas sold in Singapore. Values represent a mid-range estimate for a standard sweetened green soda.

gi_source.title: Fanta, orange, carbonated soft drink — GI 68; and sucrose-based sweetened carbonated beverages GI range 59–68
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai Iced Green Soda. GI is estimated at 63 based on analogous sweetened carbonated soft drinks in the University of Sydney GI Database (e.g. Fanta orange GI 68, cola-type drinks GI ~59–63). Thai green soda is a sucrose-syrup-based carbonated drink with approximately 10 g carbohydrate per 100 g. GI of 63 represents a conservative mid-range estimate for a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage. GL per serving = (63 × 30 g carbohydrate per 300 ml serving) / 100 = 18.9.

hypertension.guidance: Thai Iced Green Soda contains approximately 7 mg sodium per 100 g (21 mg per 300 ml serving), which is negligible from a blood pressure management perspective; however, the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain, which is an indirect risk factor for hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content and medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Thai Iced Green Soda provides approximately 30 g of free sugars per 300 ml serving with a glycemic load of approximately 18.9, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; consumption should be avoided or strictly limited as part of a carbohydrate-controlled dietary plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia and metabolic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Thai Iced Green Soda is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Iced Green Soda (Thai: โซดาเขียวเย็น, romanized: Soda Khiao Yen) is a vibrant, sweet, and refreshing carbonated soft drink popular in Thai restaurants and street food settings, including in Singapore. It is made by combining carbonated water with a sweetened green-coloured syrup, which is typically flavoured with artificial or natural fruit essences and sometimes a hint of herbal notes such as pandan or lime. The drink is served over ice, giving it a bright, jewel-green appearance and a pleasantly fizzy, sweet taste with a light herbal or fruity finish. It is an ultra-processed beverage with no significant protein or fat content. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 40 kcal, 10 g of carbohydrate (almost entirely free sugars), negligible sodium, and no dietary fibre. A standard 300 ml serving delivers approximately 120 kcal and 30 g of free sugars, placing it in the high-concern category for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its medium glycemic index (estimated 63) and glycemic load of approximately 18.9 per serving. It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, it is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular risk through dietary sugar restriction. In Singapore, it is commonly served as a side beverage at Thai casual dining restaurants such as Saap Saap Thai.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai Iced Green Soda exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for generic fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drinks, which are compositionally analogous. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for sweetened carbonated beverages. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis rather than an exact source match.
notes: Thai Iced Green Soda is a sweetened carbonated beverage. protein_g is confirmed zero as this is a sugar-syrup-based carbonated soft drink containing no protein by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central analogous carbonated soft drink entries. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in USDA FoodData Central for generic carbonated soft drinks and are not expected to be present in meaningful quantities in this product type. glycemic_index is estimated at 63 (medium category) based on analogous sweetened carbonated beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Thai green soda was found. GL per 300 ml serving = (63 × 30 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 18.9. The green colour is typically achieved with food-grade colouring; some formulations may use pandan or lime flavouring. Allergen status assumes standard formulation without milk or gluten-containing additives; individual restaurant formulations may vary. Served at Saap Saap Thai, Funan, Singapore (field survey data source record GD001583).
